stem: In right triangle XYZ, angle Y is a right angle. Point W lies on hypotenuse XZ such that YW is perpendicular to XZ. If XW = 4 and YW = 8, what is the length of WZ?
rationale: When an altitude is drawn from the right angle to the hypotenuse of a right triangle, it divides the triangle into two smaller similar triangles, and the altitude is the geometric mean of the two segments of the hypotenuse: YW² = XW · WZ. Therefore 8² = 4 · WZ, so WZ = 64/4 = 16.
